entries.title: Public Preview: Azure SQL updates for mid-March 2026
entries.summary: In mid-March 2026, the following updates and enhancements were made to Azure SQL: Reduce the consumption of storage space, I/O, and memory and improve performance without investing time and effort into index maintenance jobs. Configure automatic tuning for your databases.

entries.title: General availability: Azure SQL updates for mid-March 2026
entries.summary: In mid-March 2026, the following updates and enhancements were made to Azure SQL: Publish SQL database projects directly from Visual Studio Code to streamline database deployment workflows. View and edit SQL table data directly in the MSSQL extension.

entries.title: Generally Available: Foundry Agent Service
entries.summary: Announcing general availability (GA) of the next-gen Foundry Agent Service—a redesigned API format and runtime experience designed to help teams build and operate agents that can move from prototype to production with confidence.

entries.title: Public Preview: Azure Databricks OneLake Catalog Federation
entries.summary: You can now enable OneLake catalog federation in Azure Databricks to query Microsoft Fabric OneLake data without copying or moving it. By connecting Unity Catalog to a OneLake catalog, Azure Databricks automatically syncs schemas and tables.

entries.title: Retirement: Azure Sphere will be retired on July 31, 2031
entries.summary: The Azure Sphere service is retiring and will stop supporting all customer application, OS, bug and security updates, along with DAA certificate issuance, on July 31, 2031. Azure Sphere pioneered secure IoT connectivity.

entries.title: Retirement: Azure SQL Elastic query - Shard_Map_Manager mode
entries.summary: Starting March 31, 2027 Elastic query using Shard_Map_Manager external data source type reaches end of support. Existing workloads can continue to run, but will no longer receive updates or support.
